objective: Add a lightweight preflight automation entrypoint for fork-local RunPod redeploy validation with cheap defaults and optional heavier checks.
scope: Add root preflight.sh with strict-mode low-cost checks (py_compile, JSON parse, docker bake print), optional --with-build docker build, optional --model runtime-arg preflight via src.engine_args.get_engine_args, and document usage in README; no upstream interactions.
acceptance_criteria: ./preflight.sh runs low-cost checks by default and prints clear PASS/FAIL status per step.
--with-build runs docker build --platform linux/amd64 -t nibble-worker-vllm:preflight . as an optional costly step.
--model <HF_MODEL_ID> executes a runtime-arg preflight using get_engine_args with MODEL_NAME set and exits non-zero on exception.
--help prints usage and explains low-cost vs costly checks.
README documents quick and full preflight commands near build/deploy guidance.
tests_expected: python -c "import json; json.load(open('aiwf.request.json')); json.load(open('.runpod/hub.json')); print('json ok')"
rollback_plan: Fork-local rollback is a single commit revert removing preflight.sh and README/request updates, restoring prior redeploy workflow without preflight automation.
deploy_plan: Use ./preflight.sh as a required local gate before RunPod redeploy; reserve --with-build for release candidates or image/runtime changes, then proceed with conservative rollout 1->2->4.
threat_model: Primary risks are false confidence from partial checks and accidental costly local builds; mitigations are explicit low-cost default, clear help text, PASS/FAIL output, and optional opt-in heavy build/model checks.
